Step 1: Identify the prime numbers between 20 and 30.
The prime numbers between 20 and 30 are 23, 29.

Step 2: Determine which of these prime numbers leaves a remainder of 5 when divided by 8.
For \( n = 23 \):
\[ 23 \div 8 = 2 \text{ remainder } 7 \]
So, 23 does not leave a remainder of 5 when divided by 8.

For \( n = 29 \):
\[ 29 \div 8 = 3 \text{ remainder } 5 \]
So, 29 leaves a remainder of 5 when divided by 8.

Step 3: Conclude that the value of \( n \) is 29.
The value of \( n \) is \(\boxed{29}\).

Step 1: Identify the prime numbers between 20 and 30.
The prime numbers in this range are 23, 29.

Step 2: Determine which of these prime numbers, when divided by 8, gives a remainder of 5.
We will check each prime number:
- For \( n = 23 \):
  \[
  23 \div 8 = 2 \text{ remainder } 7 \quad \text{(since } 23 = 8 \cdot 2 + 7\text{)}
  \]
  Thus, 23 does not satisfy the condition.

- For \( n = 29 \):
  \[
  29 \div 8 = 3 \text{ remainder } 5 \quad \text{(since } 29 = 8 \cdot 3 + 5\text{)}
  \]
  Thus, 29 satisfies the condition.

Step 3: Conclude that the value of \( n \) is 29.

\[
\boxed{29}
\]
